summary: Verified Pairwise BT ranking fix for eLife Cancer Biology dual-dimension dataset. All 3 BT ranking tables (Aggregate, Significance, Strength) now display DISTINCT correlation values and different human rank orderings. The fix ensures Aggregate uses composite scores instead of only significance scores.
passed_tests: 3 distinct BT ranking panels render: 'Pairwise BT — Aggregate', 'Pairwise BT — Significance', 'Pairwise BT — Strength'
Aggregate correlation shows Spearman ρ = +0.370 (different from sig 0.302 and str 0.438)
Human rank orderings differ between dimensions (Aggregate H1 != Significance H1)
API /api/validation/pairwise-results returns dim_correlations with separate significance and strength data
API returns is_aggregate=true for aggregate correlation confirming composite calculation
critical_code_review_comments: ValidationPage.jsx lines 476-555: Correctly renders 3 BT panels when dim_correlations exists
Backend validation.py correctly computes aggregate composite score from normalized sig+str BT scores (lines 1860-1898)
Each dimension table correctly shows its own comparison array with distinct human rankings

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 3 BT panels render with distinct values)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Pairwise BT ranking fix verified. For dual-dimension datasets (eLife), the validation page now shows 3 distinct BT panels: Aggregate (composite), Significance, and Strength. Each has different correlation values against human rankings. The key fix was ensuring Aggregate uses composite scores from both dimensions rather than just significance scores.
